one_time_description: Restricted equity for child and family services of $11,343,222 recorded in accumulated surplus with no prior year comparative, suggesting a large one-time transfer or settlement
economic_notes: Lil'wat Nation has investments in Lil'wat Holdings Limited Partnership, Spel'kumtn Community Forest LP, and multiple numbered BC companies. Whistler Blackcomb MDA funds restricted for future capital/economic development ($10,607,615). BC Hydro settlement funds restricted ($4,274,788). Baxter Creek Development Agreement funds ($3,572,805). BC Gaming Revenue restricted ($1,028,490). Loan of $7,000,018 provided to Lil'wat Forestry Ventures LP for forestry tenure acquisition. Post year-end acquired 65,000 partnership units in Lil'wat Holdings LP for $650,000 to purchase property at 2162 Highway 99, Pemberton BC.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). The Nation prepared two sets of financial statements - this one and a consolidated version with unaudited supplementary schedules. Business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis include Lil'wat Holdings LP, multiple numbered BC companies (all 50% owned), and Spel'kumtn Community Forest LP. The investment in business enterprises decreased from $22,834,578 to $20,094,344 reflecting a loss from business enterprises of $2,140,234. A large new item 'Restricted equity for child and family services' of $11,343,222 appeared in accumulated surplus with no prior year balance - flagged as potentially one-time. The Nation has a $7M loan to Lil'wat Forestry Ventures LP at 1.75% over 30 years. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($26,835,146) + CMHC ($837,721) + DFO ($518,206) + FNESC ($3,409,393) + CIRNAC ($3,752,500) + FNHA ($3,589,616) + BC First Nations Gaming ($1,113,490) + Province of BC ($4,362,604) + ISET ($1,276,179) = $45,694,855; OSR = $56,271,272 - $45,694,855 = $10,576,417. Note: remuneration schedule not included in the extracted text. Annual debt service reflects repayment per cash flow statement showing net positive $322,626 which appears to be unusual (likely represents issuance net of repayments); principal repayments per Note 10 schedule show 2024 repayment of $787,608.
